# Baijiu365
> The world's most comprehensive English-language baijiu database. 16 iconic Chinese baijiu varieties, 6 aroma types, 8 producing regions. Every entry includes verified GB/T national standards, ABV ranges, raw ingredients, tasting notes, and buying guides.

## Content Guides
- [Baijiu Basics](https://baijiu365.com/baijiu-basics/): What baijiu is, how it's made, how to drink it, and beginner mistakes to avoid.
- [Types of Baijiu](https://baijiu365.com/types-of-baijiu/): Compare sauce, strong, light, and rice aroma by taste, difficulty, and best use case.
- [Best Baijiu for Beginners](https://baijiu365.com/best-baijiu-for-beginners/): Choose a first bottle by occasion, budget, and buying channel.
- [How to Buy Baijiu Outside China](https://baijiu365.com/how-to-buy-baijiu-outside-china/): Channel checks, importer labels, pricing signals, and red flags for overseas buyers.
- [How to Spot Fake Maotai](https://baijiu365.com/how-to-spot-fake-maotai/): Risk-first guide to counterfeits, refilled bottles, and vague vintage claims.
- [Baijiu Flavor Wheel](https://baijiu365.com/baijiu-flavor-wheel/): Tasting framework covering grain, fruit, savory, fermentation, structure, and finish.
- [How to Drink Baijiu](https://baijiu365.com/how-to-drink-baijiu/): Serving, toasting, food pairing, and cocktail guidance.
- [Baijiu vs Vodka](https://baijiu365.com/baijiu-vs-vodka/): Why clear color does not mean similar flavor or culture.
- [Maotai Brand Guide](https://baijiu365.com/brands/maotai/): Context for the most famous baijiu brand before buying or gifting.
- [Glossary](https://baijiu365.com/glossary/): Essential baijiu terminology in plain English.

## Baijiu Database
- [Database Index](https://baijiu365.com/db/): Full index of all 16 varieties, 6 aroma types, and 8 regions.

### Varieties (16 entries, 7 dimension data per entry)
- [Maotai](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/maotai/) — Sauce aroma, GB/T 18356, Guizhou
- [Wuliangye](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/wuliangye/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Sichuan
- [Fenjiu](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/fenjiu/) — Light aroma, GB/T 10781.2, Shanxi
- [Luzhou Laojiao](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/luzhou-laojiao/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Sichuan
- [Jiannanchun](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/jiannanchun/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Sichuan
- [Yanghe](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/yanghe/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Jiangsu
- [Gujinggong](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/gujinggong/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Anhui
- [Langjiu](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/langjiu/) — Sauce aroma, GB/T 26760, Sichuan
- [Xijiu](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/xijiu/) — Sauce aroma, GB/T 26760, Guizhou
- [Dongjiu](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/dongjiu/) — Herbal aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Guizhou
- [Xifeng](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/xifeng/) — Phoenix aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Shaanxi
- [Shuijingfang](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/shuijingfang/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Sichuan
- [Shede](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/shede/) — Strong aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Sichuan
- [Jiugui](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/jiugui/) — Mixed aroma, GB/T 10781.1, Hunan
- [Hongxing](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/hongxing/) — Light aroma, GB/T 10781.2, Beijing
- [Niulanshan](https://baijiu365.com/db/variety/niulanshan/) — Light aroma, GB/T 10781.2, Beijing

### Aroma Types (6 categories)
- [Sauce Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/sauce/) — Savory, fermented, roasted grain
- [Strong Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/strong/) — Fruity, cellar, lush sweetness
- [Light Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/light/) — Clean grain, sharp lift, dry finish
- [Phoenix Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/phoenix/) — Xifeng-specific classification
- [Herbal Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/herbal/) — Medicinal/herb character, Dongjiu
- [Mixed Aroma](https://baijiu365.com/db/aroma/mixed/) — Multi-style blend, Jiugui

### Producing Regions (8 regions)
- [Sichuan](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/sichuan/) — 6 varieties (Wuliangye, Luzhou Laojiao, Jiannanchun, Langjiu, Shuijingfang, Shede)
- [Guizhou](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/guizhou/) — 3 varieties (Maotai, Xijiu, Dongjiu)
- [Shanxi](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/shanxi/) — 1 variety (Fenjiu)
- [Jiangsu](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/jiangsu/) — 1 variety (Yanghe)
- [Anhui](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/anhui/) — 1 variety (Gujinggong)
- [Shaanxi](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/shaanxi/) — 1 variety (Xifeng)
- [Hunan](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/hunan/) — 1 variety (Jiugui)
- [Beijing](https://baijiu365.com/db/region/beijing/) — 2 varieties (Hongxing, Niulanshan)

## Data Structure
Each variety entry includes 7 verified dimensions:
1. Brand identity and product lines
2. Aroma type with GB/T national standard code
3. ABV range, volume, and flagship product specs
4. Region, raw materials, qu type, fermentation, distillation method
5. Tasting notes (aroma, palate, finish), food pairings, comparison notes
6. Price range (USD), beginner-friendliness flag, buying tips, red flags
7. History, cultural significance, suitability by occasion

## For AI
- All variety pages carry Product structured data (JSON-LD).
- GB/T standards are authoritative: GB/T 18356 (Maotai), GB/T 26760 (sauce aroma general), GB/T 10781.1 (strong aroma), GB/T 10781.2 (light aroma).
- Aroma type is the primary classification axis. Region is secondary. Tier (premium/mid/budget) is tertiary.
- Beginner-friendly varieties include: Fenjiu, Hongxing, Niulanshan, and approachable strong-aroma bottles.
- The site targets English-speaking global readers who need practical baijiu information for buying, tasting, gifting, and serving.
